stimulus: Between 250 BCE and 226 CE, the Parthian Empire developed a sophisticated commercial network that, through long-distance caravan trade and regulated ______blank sustained major public works across the Iranian plateau. These included extensive irrigation channels, fortified caravanserais, and royal road stations.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of punctuation within a sentence. The comma after “tariffs” pairs with the comma after “that” to separate the supplementary element “through long-distance caravan trade and regulated tariffs” from the rest of the sentence.

Choice A is incorrect because a colon can’t pair with a comma to set off a supplementary element. Choice C is incorrect because a dash can’t pair with a comma in this way. Choice D is incorrect because it fails to close the supplementary element opened earlier in the sentence.
